pattern: The whole array is the candidate; only when its XOR collapses to zero must you drop a single nonzero element, and only the all-zero array is hopeless.
hints: Start by asking what the XOR of every element together is.
If that total is already nonzero you keep everything; if it is zero, removing one well-chosen element can break the cancellation.
When the total XOR is zero, dropping any single nonzero element makes the remaining XOR nonzero, so the answer is n-1 unless every element is zero, in which case no subsequence works.

edge_inputs.description: two equal values cancel, drop one
total xor zero but mixed with zeros
total xor zero, must drop exactly one nonzero

stress.description: random values including zeros so both the keep-all and drop-one branches trigger
